title: Fay

{{Quote|She was the strongest of us all, and for a brief moment, I could feel ''how'' strong. I suspect she would have lived forever, if not for this one sacrifice.|Obi-Wan Kenobi|<ref name="Star Wars Republic 53: Blast Radius" />}}
'''Fay''' was a centuries-old, [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[near-Human]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] who was known to have had an intensely strong connection with [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] which allowed her to retain a youthful appearance. She was one of several legendary wandering [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Masters whose exploits were known throughout the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]].

Not believing that she was destined to teach, she left the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core]] and began roaming the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|worlds]]. For many [[Standard year/Legends|centuries]] Fay allowed the Force to guide her to where she was needed, and as a result, was known to have resolved many conflicts, and even ended entire wars.

During the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] she felt the Force guide her to the planet [[Queyta]], where she rendezvoused with a number of other Jedi Masters, including [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], to recover a vital [[Antidote/Legends|antidote]] needed for a [[Swamp gas|deadly new chemical weapon]]. The [[Mission to Queyta|ensuing mission]] resulted in Fay and the other Jedi encountering the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Asajj Ventress/Legends|Asajj Ventress]] and [[Durge/Legends|Durge]], a 2,000-year-old [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]]. During the confrontation, Fay fought with Ventress, but following an intense explosion, Ventress severely wounded the Jedi Master. Fay gave her last remaining strength to Kenobi so he could escape with the antidote, and thereby save millions of lives.

==Biography==
===Early life===
{{Quote|The Force kept her alive for many hundreds of years, Obi-Wan. And in that time, she saved many thousands of lives.|Mace Windu, to Obi-Wan Kenobi|<ref name="Star Wars Republic 53: Blast Radius" />}}
[[File:Fay-Republic53.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Jedi Master Fay]]
The [[near-Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] Fay lived during the last [[Standard year/Legends|centuries]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. A [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]], she passed the [[Jedi Trials/Legends|Jedi Trials]] and became a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]], after which she felt that the Force was calling her to lead a nomadic life away from the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core]]. After attaining the rank of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]], she decided to leave the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] and refused to take a [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], choosing instead to roam [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] wherever the Force guided her. Throughout the following centuries she rarely had reason to travel back to the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]], preferring to go where she felt she was needed.<ref name="The Official Star Wars Fact File 128" />

As time passed, and Fay was seen less and less on Coruscant, her exploits in the far corners of the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] soon took on a legendary status among many [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] students. However, a number of the older Masters knew that the legends were actually real, and they paid close attention when reports were received from far flung [[Planet/Legends|planets]] of deeds done by a mysterious Jedi matching Fay's description.<ref name="The Official Star Wars Fact File 128" />

Her time in the field, whether it was on the most remote rural planets or those closer to the Core, helped save thousands of lives. She was known to have resolved many conflicts and wars by flooding peace and love into the [[Heart/Legends|hearts]] of those gripped by hatred. Throughout the hundreds of years that she mediated wars on any number of planets, she never once used a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] in [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|physical combat]]—indeed, it was believed that after her [[Jedi training/Legends|initial training]], she never even carried one.<ref name="The Official Star Wars Fact File 128" />

===Mission to Queyta===
{{Quote|You are no Jedi, girl…you are simply hatred and bile given form.|Fay, to Asajj Ventress|<ref name="Star Wars Republic 53: Blast Radius" />}}
[[File:Fay.jpg|thumb|200px|Fay uses the Force against Ventress.]]
During the early [[Standard month/Legends|months]] of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], Fay felt that the Force was guiding her to the [[Volcano/Legends|volcanic]] planet [[Queyta]]. Once there, she realized that several other nomadic Jedi had felt the call of the Force as well—she met with Jedi Masters [[Nico Diath]], [[Jon Antilles]], [[Knol Ven'nari]], and [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], who had been involved with the war since its beginning. Kenobi was the last Jedi to arrive on the planet, and Ven'nari berated him for being late, saying that she had better places to be. Fay, however, could sense the pain and tragedy which Kenobi had already suffered thus far in the war, and reached out her hand to his forehead and filled him with a new sense of hope and energy.<ref name="The Official Star Wars Fact File 128" />

The Jedi's [[Mission to Queyta|mission]] was to infiltrate a [[Separatist Queyta base|chemical factory]] and acquire the [[Antidote/Legends|antidote]] to a new [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]] biological weapon known as [[swamp gas]]. However, upon entering the factory, Diath commented that there were no [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]]—Fay and the other Jedi heightened their Force awareness as Kenobi led the search for the antidote. The search, though, came to an abrupt halt when Fay and others encountered the [[Dark Acolyte/Legends|Dark Acolyte]] [[Asajj Ventress/Legends|Asajj Ventress]] and the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Durge/Legends|Durge]].<ref name="Star Wars Republic 53: Blast Radius" />

Immediately, Durge caused a huge explosion, and the ensuing fight saw the [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] hunter [[Death/Legends|kill]] Masters Ven'nari, Diath, and Antilles. After witnessing the death of Nico Diath, Fay realized that Ventress was trying to escape with the antidote. To stop her, Fay used the Force to [[Telekinesis/Legends|hurl]] debris and broken glass at the fleeing Ventress, causing her to falter due to her wounds. Even though Ventress tried to crush Fay's heart with the Force, Fay was able to combat the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi's]] attempts and use the Force to pull away all of Ventress's thoughts, causing her to faint. After Ventress had been neutralized, Kenobi advised Fay that he had recovered a vial of the antidote, and together the two Jedi started to withdraw in the wake of the factory's destruction.<ref name="Star Wars Republic 53: Blast Radius" />

===One with the Force===
{{Quote|Too late…you ''will'' escape…you ''will'' live…and I go to join our brothers and sisters…finally.|Fay, to Obi-Wan Kenobi|<ref name="Star Wars Republic 53: Blast Radius" />}}
[[File:FayDeath-Republic53.jpg|thumb|left|280px|Fay receives a mortal wound from Asajj Ventress.]]
The chemical factory had begun devolving into chaos due to several large explosions initiated by Durge. As Fay and Kenobi attempted to escape the compound, a large explosion occurred near the Jedi, causing Fay to falter and drop her guard. During those few moments, a revived Ventress used her [[Asajj Ventress's lightsabers/Legends|double lightsabers]] to pierce Fay from behind, leaving her severely wounded. Ventress and Durge realized that the factory was near its final destruction and boarded a nearby Separatist [[Starship/Legends|ship]], leaving Kenobi and Fay to die.<ref name="Star Wars Republic 53: Blast Radius" />

Even though the wounds she had received from Ventress were severe, Fay's strong connection with the Force would have allowed her to eventually recover.<ref name="The Official Star Wars Fact File 128" /> However, she recognized the dire need for the Republic to have the antidote that Kenobi possessed, and in a final act of self-sacrifice, touched Kenobi and filled him through the Force with her last reserves of strength. Due to Fay's sacrifice, Kenobi was able to [[Force jump/Legends|jump]] to safety and ultimately escape the planet to bring the antidote back to the Jedi Temple on Coruscant.<ref name="Star Wars Republic 53: Blast Radius" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|In all her long decades, she never raised her lightsaber in combat. I don't even know if she ''carried'' one…|Obi-Wan Kenobi, commenting on Fay|<ref name="Star Wars Republic 53: Blast Radius" />}}
[[File:Fay_holo.jpg|thumb|150px|A holographic memorial in honor of Fay.]]
Among all the [[Species/Legends|species]] who encountered her, Fay was regarded as a striking woman,<ref name="The Official Star Wars Fact File 128" /> and Jedi Master [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]] once described her as "beautiful and ageless" after an encounter with the Jedi Master. Following the mission to Queyta, Obi-Wan Kenobi commented that no one hated Master Fay, and that throughout her entire Jedi career she never felt the need to use a lightsaber in combat.<ref name="Star Wars Republic 53: Blast Radius" /> For many centuries, she roamed the galaxy while being guided by the Force—the physical presence of her personality, combined with her wisdom in the Force, allowed Fay the opportunity to bring peace and harmony to many war-torn planets she encountered.<ref name="The Official Star Wars Fact File 128" />

Fay was distinguished by her pale skin, blond [[Hair/Legends|hair]], and her pointy [[Ear/Legends|ears]] which resembled those of the [[Sephi/Legends|Sephi]]. She also had distinctive [[Tattoo/Legends|tattoos]] on her left cheek and forehead. Due to her deep connection with the Force, Fay's physical body was ageless, and she appeared to be a beautiful twenty-year-old almost her entire life.<ref name="The Official Star Wars Fact File 128" />

==Behind the scenes==
Fay's first and only appearance was in [[Republic 53|''Republic'' 53]], the fifty-third issue in the ''[[Star Wars: Republic]]'' series of [[Star Wars comic books|comics]]. Her character and story were conceived by writer [[W. Haden Blackman|Haden Blackman]] and penciled by artist [[Brian Ching]].<ref name="Star Wars Republic 53: Blast Radius" />

The character of Fay also shares characteristics with near-human individuals such as [[Aleco Stusea]], an unnamed member of the [[Wardens of the Sky]], and [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Lourdes]]. All these characters have pointy ears. [[Leland Chee]], on the [[StarWars.com Message Boards|Star Wars Message Boards]], stated that his theory is that "all pointy-eared species are either Sephi or closely related to Sephi."<ref name="TT">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=1395#16675997|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Tasty Taste]]|date=2007-06-08|time=6:48 AM}}</ref>
